sprint: Traveller Gateway / Creator Signature — Five-Item Bug Batch
task: Fixed five real, user-reported bugs in the Traveller Gateway and Creator Signature flow together: (1) the tap-to-skip hint had no explicit z-index, so the decline-to-Traveller restart's freshly-mounted Gate video (appended after the hint) silently painted over it -- fixed with an explicit z-index above the Gate video layer, verified visually. (2) a correct Creator Signature recognition used to still play the full Lumo flying-in/landing/greeting/flying-off cinematic a second time -- now skips straight to the closing flash + hand-off the instant recognition succeeds, verified to complete in ~9ms after the success callback instead of tens of seconds. (3) declining to 'Continue as a Traveller' mid-session (after the new-session My-Projects clear had already run-or-skipped) left a previous Creator's own local projects visible in the new Traveller's project list -- CreatorProjectStore.clearAll() is now called at the exact moment identity is relinquished, verified to empty the list even when the previous-Creator-project seed predates this exact decline. (4) 'inner studio screen can never load before home screen' -- index.html's <body> now starts with creation-flow-active present in the raw HTML (hiding #app from the very first byte of parse, before any script has even run), and js/app.js's _beginBoot() -- the one function every real boot path (Gateway hand-off or its fallback) funnels through -- is the single place that removes it, either immediately followed by CreationFlow.start() re-adding it (zero paint gap) or left removed only behind the restore-session modal's own translucent backdrop (unchanged, correct pre-existing behaviour); verified via continuous fast-interval polling across three full real boot paths (fresh Traveller, Returning Creator success, and the restore-modal path) confirming #app was never visible with the class absent for the Gateway's own full ~60+ second runtime. (5) a wrong tap on the Creator Signature sky-recognition grid let a child's rapid follow-up taps land during the 1100ms regenerate window, silently eating what should have been their next try -- the grid now sets pointer-events:none for that whole window (verified directly: Playwright's own click call reports the browser genuinely refusing to deliver a second click, 'intercepts pointer events', not just a JS-level guard), releasing correctly afterward with tries decrementing exactly once per real wrong tap. All 5 fixes verified individually via new Playwright scripts plus a full regression pass across the existing sky-challenge/Gateway/lumo-voice suite and both goldenBuild.js suites (World Builder v1 and v2, unaffected -- these fixes touch zero Builder files). Confined to js/gatewaySequence.js, js/magicCardUI.js, js/app.js, css/style.css, and index.html.
